prompt: An architectural diagram showing three horizontal layers stacked vertically. Top layer: three connected nodes representing identity concepts — a fingerprint icon, a building icon, and a handshake icon — linked with clean lines. Middle layer: four nodes in a row representing transaction domains — a shopping cart icon, a paintbrush icon, an antenna icon, and a speech bubble icon — each distinct. Bottom layer: a single wide node with a shield icon that has dotted connection lines reaching up to every node in the transaction layer, showing it operates across all domains. Clean arrows flow downward from the top through the middle. Teal and emerald color palette. The diagram feels architectural and organized — a clear system map. Absolutely no text, no words, no labels, no letters anywhere in the image.
alt_text: Three-layer architecture diagram showing identity layer (brand, registry, accounts) at top, transaction domains (media buy, creative, signals, sponsored intelligence) in the middle, and governance as a cross-cutting layer connecting to all transaction domains

prompt: A four-tier ecosystem diagram using only icons, no words. Top tier: two human figures shaking hands — one with a briefcase, one at a storefront awning. Second tier: a central hub connected to three smaller hubs via clean lines — each smaller hub has a distinct icon (antenna, storefront, palette). Third tier: two connected technical shapes — a circuit board shape linked to a server rack shape. Fourth tier spanning full width: a shield icon next to a human figure with clipboard, both connected by dotted lines to every tier above. The tiers are clearly separated with space between them, flowing top to bottom. Teal and emerald color palette on white background. Absolutely no text, no words, no labels, no letters anywhere in the image.
alt_text: Four-tier ecosystem diagram showing business principals at top, orchestration layer with specialized agents in the middle, technical execution below, and governance with human oversight spanning all layers
